setting.note: Philadelphia and the making of a financier. Dreiser builds Frank Cowperwood's rise and ruin out of the real ledgers of the city: the banks and brokerages of Third Street, the trading floor of the exchange, the municipal offices in Independence Hall he corrupts, and the great gray penitentiary on Fairmount where he is jailed. It brings Philadelphia into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Theodore Dreiser, first published 1912; the author died in 1945.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US (published 1912) and the UK/EU (author died 1945).
rights.attribution: Theodore Dreiser, The Financier (1912). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The gold of coin and balance, taken from the ten-dollar gold piece young Cowperwood is handed. It suits the finance theme and clears WCAG AA on paper.
